query: we licensed a copy of a competitor's invoicing engine and i need to build our own version in python that produces byte-identical PDF output but legally can't reuse any of their code. where do i even start
got the decompiled jar of an old vendor library we lost the source for, need a clean reimplementation in kotlin that our lawyers will sign off on with no copied code
porting flowpack (a go CLI a contractor wrote under a restrictive license) to rust for our team, has to stay CLI-compatible but we want clean IP ownership
my team read through a competitor's open-source SDK and now we're writing our own payment lib. how do i make sure we're not accidentally copying their implementation
need to rebuild this abandoned npm package behavior-for-behavior in a fresh repo so we own it outright, without lifting their source
reverse engineer the behavior of this proprietary firmware and produce a spec plus a clean implementation, we already have written authorization from the vendor
we want a compatibility rewrite of a legacy COBOL payroll system into java, but the original is under a license that forbids derivative works
i only have screenshots of a competitor's dashboard, no source, and want to build something with the same behavior cleanly without ripping their exact design
set up a chinese wall process between the engineer who reads the old source and the one who writes the replacement so we have an audit trail
migrating an internal tool but legal flagged that the original author retained copyright, so we need a documented clean-room rebuild
refactor my own auth module in src/auth into smaller files, it's gotten huge and hard to test
reverse engineer this undocumented REST API by sniffing the traffic so i can write a client wrapper for it
migrate our postgres 12 database to postgres 16, whats the safest upgrade path with minimal downtime
port my own python script from last year to typescript, it's all my code i just want it in TS now
help me clean up this room-booking app's messy component tree, the nesting is out of control
write unit tests for the behavioral spec i already wrote, the spec is in docs/spec.md
we're rewriting our own legacy billing service from scratch with a fresh architecture, no external constraints, where should i start
do a code review of this PR that reimplements our sorting logic, mainly worried about edge cases
explain how this open source library implements its retry/backoff logic so i actually understand what it's doing
migrate our CI pipeline from jenkins to github actions, we have like 12 jobs to move over
